page: Amazon Web Services r8i.large is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor based on the x86_64 architecture, providing 2 vCPUs across 1 physical core with 2 threads. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz and includes a 480 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it features a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks show strong single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2163.0 and strong single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression rates. However, multi-core performance is limited due to the dual-vCPU configuration, as reflected in a weak Geekbench multi-core score of 2725.0 and a poor stress-ng multi-core CPU result. Memory performance is mixed; while the Passmark memory score falls in the average tier, large-block read-only memory bandwidth is relatively low at 20.69 GB/sec. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions for accelerated matrix operations.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r8i.large offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio within a modern DDR5 hardware class, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel compute. It is well-suited for single-threaded applications, lightweight database operations, caching layers, text processing, and basic memory-intensive enterprise applications.
description: Amazon Web Services r8i.large is a memory-optimized instance featuring an Intel Xeon 6975P-C x86_64 processor with 2 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of DDR5 memory. Operating at 3.9 GHz, this Nitro-based instance delivers strong single-core performance, highlighted by high single-threaded compression and text processing benchmarks. However, its multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities are more modest due to its dual-vCPU limit. The hardware includes advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. With a baseline network speed of 1 Gbps, the r8i.large is designed for memory-intensive, single-threaded workloads, lightweight caching, and database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8i.large is a memory-optimized instance with 2 vCPUs, 16.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, offering strong single-core performance for memory-bound workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8i.large is a memory-optimized instance featuring 2 vCPUs, 16.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A memory-optimized dual-vCPU instance featuring DDR5 RAM and strong single-core performance for memory-bound, single-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 2 vCPUs and 3.9 GHz clock speed
16.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory with 8.0 GB per core
Strong single-core and single-threaded compression performance
Supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets
No local storage and 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Best suited for memory-intensive, single-threaded applications and caching
